title: Raise error if PNG transparency has incorrect type or length when saving
author: radarhere
body:
1. When saving PNG images transparency, I suggest raising an error if the value is not of the type expected (or in the case of RGB, if the sequence length is not 3).

2. Reading the following lines,
https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow/blob/82ac16d89d83fcf43d362a39ace76195deb54dcf/src/PIL/PngImagePlugin.py#L1446-L1448
https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow/blob/82ac16d89d83fcf43d362a39ace76195deb54dcf/src/PIL/PngImagePlugin.py#L1465
I think it would be clearer to write
```python
if transparency is not None:
```
and
```python
if im.encoderinfo.get("transparency") is not None:
```
